sources.title: California Attorney General: CCPA Official Resources
IAPP: Comparing GDPR and CCPA Side-by-Side
DLA Piper: Global Data Protection Comparison Guide

result.summary: GDPR and CCPA share core principles of data subject rights and transparency but differ fundamentally in scope (GDPR applies to all data processing, CCPA to commercial sale/sharing), consent models (opt-in vs opt-out), and enforcement mechanisms.
key_findings: GDPR requires explicit opt-in consent for data processing with 6 legal bases, while CCPA defaults to opt-out for data sale/sharing and does not require consent for general data collection
GDPR's right to erasure is broader than CCPA's right to delete, covering all processing activities vs CCPA's limitation to data collected from the consumer directly

Enforcement disparity is significant: GDPR fines reached 2.1B EUR total in 2023 with dedicated DPAs in each member state, while CCPA enforcement remains primarily through the California AG with fewer than 30 formal actions
